reason: Both answers correctly identify the core dynamics: MTTR as a gameable, lagging average; distribution vs. mean (many small incidents dropping mean while total pain rises); "closing" vs. "fixing" (recurrence); definition drift/severity reclassification; survivorship of untracked chronic issues; and the fix (SLOs/error-budget burn, incident frequency, P90/P95 distribution, pager load). Both land on essentially the same actionable recommendations with similar clarity and concision. B explicitly names Goodhart's Law and adds two nice touches — validating the engineers' qualitative sentiment as legitimate data and tracking post-incident action-item completion — while A offers a concrete closing diagnostic (plot incident count and impact-minutes against MTTR) that is equally useful. The two are comparably strong with no correctness issues in either; differences are minor and roughly offsetting.
